summary: This is a very brief post where Joel notes he has returned from a weekend in Boston and observes that discussion around Netscape 6 has been overwhelmingly negative. He acknowledges the bias in the feedback, since he had specifically asked for bug reports, which naturally skews responses toward complaints.
key_points: Public reception of Netscape 6 was overwhelmingly negative
Soliciting bug reports creates selection bias toward negative feedback
Joel shows awareness that the framing of a question influences the responses you get
money_quotes: The discussion on Netscape 6 seems to be pretty universally negative.
I know, I know, I asked for bug reports, so it's not exactly fair.
key_insight: Asking for bug reports inherently biases feedback toward negativity, making it an unreliable gauge of overall reception.
